Feeder Appointment & Operations Policy
Feeder Identification and Proposal
• Responsibility: The Territory Leader Distributor (TLD) is responsible for identifying
potential locations where Distributor service is inadequate due to logistical
challenges.
• Assessment Criteria: The proposal for appointing a Feeder must be justified based on
the criteria outlined in the Feeder Proposal Matrix (refer to Section 3).
• Required Proposal Documentation: When submitting a proposal, the TLD must
include:
o The completed Feeder Proposal Matrix.
o Clear map views (e.g., from Google Maps or similar) illustrating:
▪ The precise location of the proposed Feeder.
▪ The distance between the primary Distributor's (point and the
proposed Feeder location.
▪ The proposed operational territory/area the Feeder will service.
• Approval Process:
o The TLD submits the Feeder appointment proposal, including the completed
matrix, to the Zonal Business Manager (ZBM).
o The ZBM reviews the proposal and forwards it to the General Manager (GM) -
Sales Development at the Head Office (HO) for final approval.
Feeder Proposal Matrix
(The following criteria must be evaluated and documented when proposing a Feeder)

Feeder Appointment and Product Scope
• Final Authority: Feeder appointments require prior approval from the GM - Sales
Development (HO).
• Permitted Products: Feeders are authorized to be billed for and distribute only the
following product categories: 2/3 Wheeler tyres & tubes, PCR/UVR tyres & tubes,
LCV tyres & tubes, and LM tyres & tubes.
Feeder Business Commitment
TLDs have to also to fill in the feeder business commitment as per the below table. The table
captures the committed MC volumes for the 3 quarters post appointment.
Feeder Name Q (Quarter of Q + 1 Q + 2 Q + 3
Appointment)
Operational Guidelines and Responsibilities
• Territory Demarcation: Feeders must operate strictly within their assigned and
demarcated territory.
• Market Expansion: Feeders are expected to actively expand the retail network and
explore alternative sales channels, including battery shops, spare parts dealers,
lubrication outlets, OE workshops, and fuel stations.

records.text:
• Order & Payment Collection: The Feeder is responsible for collecting orders and
payments from all attached retailers.
• Billing Records: Feeders must provide detailed billing information upon request by
the company.
• Non-Encroachment Clause:
o Feeders must not service existing markets or customers already covered by
supplying Distributor.
o Feeders must refrain from any activities that could negatively impact the
business operations of existing Distributors in the market.
o Parties involved in the infringement of the above two clauses will have their
feeder status immediately revoked upon the final approval of “Head – Sales
Development”
• Loyalty Programs: Participation in CEAT customer loyalty programs, such as the CKS
program, is not extended to appointed Feeders.
Feeder Compensation Structure
1. Eligibility for Feeder Support:
o To qualify for Feeder Support, a Feeder must achieve average monthly sales
of at least 200 motorcycle tyres (average monthly) within a quarter.
2. Calculation of Feeder Support:
o For eligible Feeders (those meeting the criteria in point 1), the potential
support amount is calculated as 2% of the total invoice value before tax (pre-
tax BOI) for their purchases during the quarter.
3. Maximum Feeder Support Amount:
o The Feeder Support payment is capped at a maximum of INR 50,000 per
quarter, regardless of the calculated 2% amount.
4. Disbursement Process (Feeder Support):
o The calculated Feeder Support (up to the INR 50k cap) is provided by the
supplying Distributor.
o The company will then reimburse the Distributor for this amount at the end
of each quarter.

Warranty Registration

• Warranty registration for new CEAT tyres can be completed through CEAT
authorized channel partners, the website (www.ceat.com), toll-free numbers 1800-
221-213 or 1800-267-1213, and WhatsApp (8657470799).
• For warranty-registered tyres, the warranty period will be calculated from the date
of registration. Otherwise, it will be calculated from the date of manufacturing,
except for the PCR/UVR category, where warranty registration is mandatory.
• By registering the warranty, customers become eligible for an extended warranty
coverage of an additional six months beyond the standard warranty period.
• Warranty registration can be completed within one year from the date of
manufacturing for all tyres. However, for all 17 inches and above SKUs in the UVR
category, warranty registration can be completed within two years from the date of
manufacturing.
Terms and Conditions
Every tyre, tube & flap manufactured and/or marketed by “CEAT Ltd” is warranted to be free from
defects in material quality or workmanship in its first tread life & warranty period. If our examination
shows that a product has failed during the warranty period mentioned in this warranty policy, we
shall replace the product on pro-rata calculation basis subject to terms and conditions of this
warranty policy. The amount to be compensated for the tyre will be calculated from the percentage
of tread wear. In other words, the so called “remaining tread depth measurement system” will be
applied.
Calculation of prorated amount
• The credit for pro rata replacement will be calculated by multiplying the current selling price
of the Eligible Tire (exclusive of taxes & service charges) at the time of purchase, by the
percentage of utilized tread depth (as explained in the example given below).
Example:
Original tread depth of Eligible Tire 7 mm
Tread depth when presented for claim 3.5 mm

applicable taxes are not covered under this policy and shall be borne by the Customer.

o Tyres worn out beyond tread pattern (ply exposed) shall not be considered.
o Tyres with uneven/improper wear beyond the parameters as defined in annexure 3
shall not be covered.
o Tyres damaged or wear due to vehicle mechanical irregularities.
o Tyres damaged due to vehicle overload or improper inflation pressure.
o Tyres damaged on account of fire, accident, climatic conditions, improper storage or
any other external factor.
o Tyres which are re-treaded or second-hand.
o Tyres/Tubes/Flaps purchased from unauthorised dealers.
o Tyres which are damaged intentionally including Serial number tampered or buffed
etc.
o Improper use and Improper Repair.
o Contamination or degradation by petroleum products or other chemicals, fire or
water.
o Tyres which are imported
o CEAT Run Flat tyres fitted on vehicles which are not equipped with a functional
Temperature Pressure Monitoring System (TPMS) or a Low Tyre Pressure Warning
System.

o Tyres worn out beyond tread pattern (ply exposed) shall not be considered.
o Conditions for damages to tread and Sidewall/shoulder.
▪ Tread – Nail injuries and puncture on tread comes under the limitations of
being reparable and hence not considered in Policy K
▪ Shoulder & Sidewall – As any repair is not sustainable on shoulder & sidewall
region, all damages in this area will be covered in Policy K
o Tyres which only have uneven wear, misalignment/balancing wear, without any
irreparable cuts and damages, shall not be considered.
o Tyres damaged on account of fire, climatic conditions, improper storage, or any

o Tyres which are re-treaded or second-hand.
o Tyres/purchased from unauthorised dealers.
o Tyres which are damaged intentionally including Serial number tampered or buffed

o Contamination or degradation by petroleum products or other chemicals, fire or

o RFT (Run-flat tyres) are not covered under this policy
o Tyres which are imported

• Stepney tyres (identified as ‘STEPNEE’ on the tyre sidewall), which are supplied as part of the
original equipment with the vehicle, are excluded from coverage under both the Standard
Warranty Policy and the Unconditional Warranty Policy.
• The following Puncture Clause will be applicable for Warranty and claim processing in PCUV
& 2W tyres for puncture on tread:
o No Puncture clause (no. of Punctures) applicable up to 25% of the tread wear
o Maximum 6 punctures allowed after 25% tread wear up to end of life.
• The following clause will be applicable for any Run Flat case in LCV bias /Radial (2030/2040)
any SKU.
o Customer must present CEAT Tubes along with Tyres for claim settlement.
o Only CEAT Fitted tube will be considered along with tyres, else will be rejected.
o Tubes will be adjusted on pro rata basis against run flat (As per Policy K up to 50%
Tread Wear).
o The tube should match with the claim tyre.
• In Puncture Safe 2W tyres, all nail cuts lesser than 2.5mm will be adjusted under Accepty,
while all Nail cuts greater than 2.5mm will be adjusted in Policy k, provided all the other
warranty conditions are met as mentioned in this document.
Wobbling for 2-wheeler tyre
• Any tyre with wobbling complaint to be adjusted under technical warranty (If Tyre wear is
within 15%), and under special warranty (If tyre wear is between 15% to 50% wear).
• Wobbling complaints can only be adjusted if they meet the following conditions.
o Tyre has no uneven/one side wear.
o Tyre should not have any cut or puncture repair.
o Vehicle should not have mechanical irregularities.
• Tyres with road hazard damages such as through cuts, external injuries, impact break will be
considered only under special warranty policy as per the directives mentioned in Annexure
2 (Policy.k).
• Warranty on Sludge invoiced tyres - Normal Warranty is applicable on all tyres billed under
sludge (ZSLG) invoice as per policy.
o No warranty on New Defective (ND) billed under sludge (ZSLG) invoice.
• Disposition Authority - All Authorized ZSM, Authorized RSMs, Authorized CFA’s, Authorized
Channel Partners or any person authorized by CEAT.

• CEAT’s liability is limited to the cost of tyres, tubes, or flaps only as per policy and does not
cover compensation for incidental or consequential damages, including vehicle downtime or
loss of time.
• The warranty is applicable for the first owner of the product and is not transferable.
• Apart from the warranty stated in the warranty policy, there are no other guarantee,
warranty, or liability, express or implied, applicable to the products of CEAT. No
representative has authority to make any other representation, promise, agreement except
as stated herein.
• The warranty is only for the product under complaint and if the tyre is accepted under
warranty the companion tyre or any other vehicles components will not be considered for
warranty.
• CEAT shall not be liable for any damage caused to any vehicle or wheel components arising
from or related to demounting, mounting, balancing, or any other tyre-related services.
• CEAT shall not be liable for any loss, injury, or damage to life or property arising out of, or in
connection with, tyre damage or any tyre-related services, whether direct, indirect,
incidental, or consequential.
• Tyres presented to an authorized CEAT Dealer under claim for warranty shall remain the
property of the consumer and CEAT accepts no responsibility for loss or damage to tyres
while they are in the custody or control of an authorized CEAT Dealer for inspection. The
tyre will be property of CEAT once adjusted by CEAT Customer Service Representative as per
the warranty policy & received at CEAT premises.
• CEAT Tyres on its own discretion may discontinue any product at any time without any
notice in advance to existing customer. In case the exact replacement for the product under
claim is not available during Warranty Period (if applicable), due to any reason, a suitable
product with available appropriate size/pattern will be offered as replacement during
warranty period. The price difference applicable for replaced product shall be borne by the
customer.
• All products with respect to which a claim has been rejected , if not collected within 21 days
of intimation of rejection to customer will be disposed off. CEAT or CEAT dealer will not be
liable for any loss due to such disposal.
• CEAT reserve the right to revise the terms and conditions of the warranty policy without any
prior notice.

This transportation can be from

• OE godown to OEM

CONDITIONS FOR Damage In Transit material must be identified during unloading
SETTLING DAMAGE IN of material from Vehicle.
TRANSIT 1. Plant to DC / DC- to CFA –
If DIT material is identified while unloading the Vehicle, it
will be notified on IODC and Drivers acknowledgement is to
be obtained from Driver of Vehicle.
The entire amount of material damaged by Transit should be
recovered from the transporter.
The DIT must be reported immediately on receipt of such
material.
If DC / CFA fails to report DIT immediately on receipt of
material
, amount will be debited to DC or CFA respectively.
2. CFA to Dealer / Distributor / Direct Fleet –
DIT should be reported at the time of receiving the material &
should be mentioned in the Invoice / LR with acknowledgement
of driver / Transporter.
In this case the DIT material will be debited to the CFA & the
amount will be recovered from the CFA.
In case it is not reported immediately, DIT will not be considered
for adjustment.
Under no circumstances material which gets damaged during
mounting / demounting will be treated as DIT.

SPK Policy & SOP-FY27
Policy:
The SPK adjustment can be done as per the following Budget:
1. Empowerment to RM & ZSM Jointly - Budget - 0.07% of Region’s
TBB + TBR Sales Value.
2. Empowerment to ZGM - Budget – 0.27% of Zone’s TBB+TBR
Sales Value.
3. Empowerment to VP-CSD - Budget - 0.01% of All India TBB+TBR
Sales Value.
SOP For Budget & Utilization
1. Budget will be quarterly based on Previous quarter’s actual
TBB+TBR (TTF) sales value (Without GST).
2. Carry forward of balance budget is allowed to next Quarter/s
within the same financial year.
3. Q1 SPK budget to be calculated on previous year’s Q4 sales
4. Each Region/Zone/Division should have clear tracking of Budget
v/s spend on monthly basis & should not exceed the spending
over budget.
5. The SPK adjustment will be jointly approved by RM & ZSM.
Approval by mail can be taken with complete details as
mentioned below.
6. Please ensure that the SPK approval should have the Complete
details of customer (as per annexure-1) with proper customer
verification by TL / RSM.
7. The SPK budget should be utilized Strictly for customer retention
& strictly NOT to be used for dealer discounting.
8. Maximum 6 tyres for any fleet can be done in a quarter.

9. This SPK budget is for both Replacement & OE customer
retention.
10. Please ensure that the tyres adjusted under SPK should
fall under the stipulated warranty period as per policy.
11. The award value will be 50% of the remaining NSD. (Ex: -
If new tyre NSD is 10mm and current/Defective NSD is 8mm
(20% wear) Award will be for 80%/2=40% prorata. (Applicable
for TBB/TBR only)

Policy:
The Test tyres can be approved as per the following Process:
1. Empowerment to ZGM & ZSM Jointly - Budget - 0.25% of Zone’s
Total Sales Quantity.
2. Empowerment to VP Sales - Budget - 0.25% of Division’s Total
Sales Quantity.
Authority Matrix
Proposer
TL/RSM (with RM / ZSM in the loop)
Approver
ZGM (with sales VP, GM-Catg, Mktg, VP-CSD in the
loop)
SOP For Budget & Approval
1. Budget will be quarterly on the base of Previous quarter’s actual
sales Quantity (Ok Tyres excluding Claim, Test & Sludge sales)
for TRUCK (TBB+TBR) & LCV Separately.
2. The Test tyres to be billed @20% discount under ZITT. No other
discount shall be applicable on these test tyres.
3. Carry forward of balance budget is not allowed to subsequent
quarters.

4. Each Zone/Division should have clear tracking of Budget v/s Billed
test tyres on monthly basis & should not exceed over budget
quantity.
5. The test tyres will be jointly approved by ZGM (Sales VP) & ZSM.
Approval over mail can be taken with complete details as per
standard format.
6. The test tyres are strictly used for customer conversion or
retention and should not be used for dealer discounting.
7. Test tyres can only be given to identified Halla Bol Fleets.
8. Addition of any new fleet must be verified by ZSM and added in
the Halla Bol data / eFleet module before the test tyre proposal
being approved.
9. TL / RSM shall send the fitment details & entered in eFleet Module.
10. In case the fitment details are not received no subsequent
allocation for test tyre to that Zone.
11. TL / RSM / ZSM to present the Performance Tracker in their
respective MORs.
12. ZM to review Tracker to understand conversion & repeat
purchase of customers who have been allocated Test Tyres,
during ZM MOR.
13. For any specific requirement of testing (NPD / Controlled
Product evaluation etc..) a separate SOP will be followed as
directed by Marketing / R&D for that specific test.

Subject: Fleet Policy, 2026-27
This Fleet Policy supersedes all previous policies on Fleet. The new policy for Direct Fleet is given below:
1. Minimum SDS for Existing SDS Fleets (Fleets having SDS as on 30th March’26) – 5 Lacs or
otherwise come to Cash & Carry.
2. All New Fleets to be appointed with 5 Lacs SDS or Cash & Carry only, unless & until approved
by the concern HO person.
3. Minimum Offtake 75 Truck Tyres in a quarter. If the minimum offtake criteria are not met,
the fleet will be blocked for all future billing, until approved by HO.
These set of Fleet (FL) will be offered special discount which will be approved as per approval matrix.
The Fleets with SDS are entitled to SDS interest of 8% (Eight percent) per annum subject to below conditions.
The SDS Interest will be payable through Credit Note generated on a Quarterly basis.
The Fleet will be eligible for interest if it does minimum one rotation of SDS in Quarter. If there is less than
one rotation in that quarter than the fleet will be eligible for interest on the value of business done during that
quarter. Fleet will not be eligible for interest if there is NIL business in that quarter.
PAN number is mandatory for SDS interest payment to the fleet.
The Interest on SDS will be paid on actual deposit in company books, calculated on daily balance basis.
Please note that Company reserves the right to revise interest rates on SDS according
to prevailing market conditions.
The fleet with SDS will be offered credit limit in the ratio of 1:1 to his security deposit. This means that, at
any point of time the total outstanding of the fleet cannot exceed the security deposit amount, unless & until
approved as per authority matrix.
Credit days are as below:

earlier.
An additional 3-day grace period, after the credit period, will be given to the fleet to pay the outstanding
invoices. If the payments are cleared within the grace period, no penal interest debit note will be raised.
However, penal interest (PDN) will be charged at the rate of 8% per annum against all the payments made
beyond the grace period. Penal debit note calculation will start from the 1st day after the credit period.
Penal interest debit note will be raised on a Quarterly basis.

Any invoice or part thereof, outstanding beyond 30 days will be automatically adjusted against credit balance,
followed by security deposit.
Similarly, any debit note generated by the company on the fleet shall be liable to be paid and any such
outstanding beyond 30 days will be adjusted against SDS interest followed by Security Deposit,
System will automatically de-activate all such accounts, where no transaction takes place for 60 days. HO can
re-activate such accounts, subject to availability of duly filled up form along with required documents.
If there is a cheque return by a fleet, 0.5% of the cheque value or Rs 1000.00, whichever is higher, will be
debited to the fleet.
If there is a single instance of cheque return, the payment term would be shifted to advance payment through
electronic payment transfer for all future cases.
Date of cheque return as accounted in Company books will be considered for all the above clauses.
Fleet Accounts without SDS deposit:
These set of Fleet (FL) will be offered special discount which will be approved as per approval matrix.
The Fleet must work on advance payment (Cash & Carry). No credit will be offered to the fleet unless & until
approved otherwise.

1. Minimum Offtake 75 Truck Tyres in a quarter. If the minimum offtake criteria are not met, the fleet

Subject: CFS Policy – 2026-27
This CFS Policy supersedes all previous policies on CFS. The new policy for CFS Fleet is given below:
For all CFS fleets (TMO, ETMA ) :
The fleets will be governed as per the terms of the contract which is already signed with the fleet
• Any modification in contract terms leading to higher than approved offer toolkit
discount can be approved by CFS HO and VP (Replacement Sales).
• Any modification in contract terms leading to lower or equal to the approved offer
toolkit discount can be approved by CFS HO.
Terms & Conditions:
1. Payment terms: Cash and Carry / Financial Bank Guarantee (BG) / Security Deposit (SDS)
2. Minimum SDS or Bank Guarantee: 5 Lac
3. Any limit to CFS fleets will have to be approved by GM (Commercial) & GM (KA).
4. The fleets with SDS are entitled to SDS interest of 8% (Eight percent) per annum. The SDS
Interest will be payable through Credit Note generated on a Quarterly basis as per following
terms:
• Base interest on SDS @ 8% p.a. is applicable to all set of active fleets irrespective
of any condition barring the consistent business clause mentioned below.
• The Fleet will be eligible for interest if it does minimum one rotation of SDS in
Quarter. If there is less than one rotation in that quarter than the fleet will be
eligible for interest on the value of business done during that quarter. Fleet will
not be eligible for interest if there is NIL business in that quarter.
• PAN number is mandatory for SDS interest payment to the fleet.
• The Interest on SDS will be paid on actual deposit in company books, calculated
on daily balance basis.
• The fleet with SDS or BG will be offered credit limit in the ratio of 1:1 to his SDS
or BG. This means that, at any point of time the total outstanding of the fleet
cannot exceed the SDS or BG amount.
• Please note that Company reserves the right to revise interest rates on SDS
according to prevailing market conditions.
5. Credit assessment is mandatory for all fleets availing credit facilities.
• No limit / extra credit days if fleet does not co-operate for credit assessment.
6. No billing to be done in case of payment overdue (OD) without approval from GM
(Commercial).
7. If credit to be provided basis the credit assessment, then it should be against PDC only.
Identification in SAP:
All direct fleets to be marked in SAP as FC with distribution code 04.
Overall toolkit discount:
For P2 direct fleets: Total toolkit discount should be within 7.6%.
Any discount between 7.6% to 8% can be approved by ZM and CFS HO.
Any discount beyond 8% will have to be approved by VP (Replacement Sales)

For P2 indirect fleets:
Total discount outflow here would be truck category weighted average dealer discount + CFS
specific expenses (manpower+ digitization etc.). Kitty for CFS specific discount would be limited
to 3.6% of pre-tax monthly commitment value.
Toolkit to only have CFS specific expenses related costs in the toolkit.
CFS expenses between 3.6% to 4% can be approved by ZM and CFS HO.
Any discount beyond 4% will have to be approved by VP (Replacement Sales)
Additional dealer deposit should be realized at the time appointment/ Contract singing for the
indirect fleet.
Indirect fleets under CFS will only be considered if there is an increase in dealer SDS and the
legal contract is uploaded in the SFDC. Following this, the Emist ID will be issues accordingly.
For P1 fleets: Total toolkit discount must be equal to or lesser than the current BOI discount
of the fleet.
Any discount higher than current BOI discount to fleet can be approved by VP (Repl Sales)
Any new conversion should have at least 30% TBR SOB as the commitment quantity (Exception
to be provided by VP Sales/CFS HO)

have to be agreed upon with the fleet in the contract.

Machinery for fleets:
CFS fleet will have the option to purchase and reimburse following machinery from CEAT approved
vendors as per following terms.
▪ For direct fleet  6.25% of machinery cost or 1.5% of quarterly sales value,
whichever is lower (pre-tax)
▪ For indirect fleet  6.25% of machinery cost or 1.5% of quarterly sales value as
per NBP, whichever is lower (pre-tax) ▪ Terms and Conditions:
▪ Reimbursement will happen quarter to quarter (Maximum up to 4 years or contract
period whichever is earlier), subject to 100% contract commitment adherence in
the quarter.
▪ Fleet must complete quarterly commitment target to be eligible for machinery
reimbursement for that quarter.
▪ Fleet has the option to recover the lost committed volume in subsequent quarter/s
in same Financial Year to be eligible for reimbursement of machinery cost.

under this scheme. The purchase request must be routed through CEAT only.

whichever is lower.

C/DIS/26-27/02
30 March 2026
CEAT PARTNERSHIP POLICY for 2026-27
1. MINIMUM BUSINESS
A. All Existing Channel Partners (appointed on/ before 29 March 2026) will
have to do a minimum business of Rs. 5.0 Lac (post tax)
B. All New Channel Partners (appointed on/ after 30 March 2026) dealing with
the Company will have to do a minimum business as follows:
a. For passenger category dealers: Rs. 5.0 Lac (Post tax) average per
month (APM) in every quarter.
b. For commercial category dealers: Rs. 7.5 Lac (Post tax) average per
month (APM) in every quarter
Applicability of clause a. or b. shall be ascertained by ZM of respective zone.
GST registration is mandatory for all channel partners.
2. SECURITY DEPOSIT
All Channel Partners dealing with the Company will have to maintain minimum
Security Deposit with the Company as follows:
A. For Existing channel partners- Higher of:
a. APM Post Tax Value Target for the financial year 26-27
b. Rs 5.0 lac
B. For New Channel Partners: Rs. 5.0/ 7.5 lac (as per Clause-1).
Below this no business shall be carried on.
3. INTEREST ON SECURITY DEPOSIT (SDS)
Channel Partners will be entitled to SDS interest of 8% (Eight percent) per
annum subject to below clauses:
i) Deduction from interest payable will be made for all Overdue Payment
for the no. of days delayed, through debit note @8% with applicable
GST. The Debit note amount will be auto adjusted against SDS
interest amount.

ii) If the cheque returns are more than 5% of the turnover in a quarter, no
Security Interest will be paid for that Quarter.
iii) Interest will be paid in line with quarterly rotation of 3.0, 2.5, 2.5 & 3.0.
If quarterly rotation is as per the norm, then 8% interest p.a. will be paid
on actual SDS. If quarterly rotation is below norm, then 8% interest p.a.
will be paid on actual sales.
The SDS Interest will be payable through Quarterly Credit Note, after
deduction of TDS.
Interest on SDS will be computed on actual deposit in company books,
calculated based on daily balance.
PAN number is mandatory for SDS interest payment to the Channel
Partners.
Please note that Company reserves the right to revise interest rates
on SDS according to prevailing market conditions
4. TRADE DISCOUNT:
Trade discount of 0.75% will be given on the body of invoice (BOI). Trade
Discount is not applicable on sludge, test tyre and NOE invoices and Warranty
and Claim.
5. CREDIT LIMIT:
The Channel Partner will be offered credit limit in the ratio of 1:1 to their
security deposit. This means that, at any point of time, the total outstanding of
the Channel Partner cannot exceed the security deposit amount.
6. CREDIT PERIOD & MODE OF COLLECTION:
Type of Channel Partner Credit Period
LOCAL 27 days
UPCOUNTRY 30 days
Credit period of Channel Partner will start from the date of dispatch or 48 hours
from the date of invoice, whichever is earlier. All cheques will be deposited in
the bank as per due date.
All new dealers shall be on electronic mode of collection only.
Channel partners will be given benefit of 10 CEAT regional holidays in
computation of due date. If due date falls on any of these 10 holidays, the due
date will be extended by 1 day.

7. PROMPT PAYMENT DISCOUNT (PPD):
All Channel Partner will be eligible for PPD on all payments made by cheque /
RTGS / Online Payment/Credit Notes and SDS adjusted against invoices, within
the credit period as mentioned in section 5.0 above.
PPD will be 1.00% of Pre-Tax value on payment received / credit note / SDS
adjusted against the invoice. It will be paid even on partial adjustment of
invoices if adjusted within the credit period.
Mode of Disbursement – Monthly Credit Note
All the Channel Partners making payment through Channel Finance will get 1%
PPD on BOI (body of invoice).
Sludge, NOE, warranty, claim & Test Tyre invoices are excluded from PPD
entitlement.
EXCEPTION:
Channel Partner can recover lost PPD and get reversal of delayed payment debit
note, upon completion of required rotations of security deposit in given quarter;
without availing any extra limit over and above the policy.
Quarter-wise required rotation: Q1-3: Q2-2.5: Q3-2.5 & Q4-3.
8. NEW CHANNEL PARTNER – ADDITIONAL BENEFITS:
New dealers (appointed on 30 March 2026 and after) shall be eligible for SDS
Interest @8% p.a. and GLD 0.75% on actual turnover unconditional for current
financial year FY27.
9. ADJUSTMENT OF OVERDUE / DEBIT NOTE:
Any invoice or part thereof, outstanding beyond 30 days from the due date will
be adjusted against SDS interest followed by security deposit. Similarly, Channel
Partner liable to pay DN generated by the company within 7 days from the date
of DN to avoid auto adjustment of the same against SDS Interest followed by
Security Deposit.
10. CHEQUE RETURNS:
If there is a cheque return by a Channel Partner, 0.5% of the cheque value or
Rs 5,000, whichever is higher, will be debited to the Channel Partners.

The payment of the returned cheque must be paid through RTGS/Online
Payment mode only & no bounced cheque will be represented in bank for
clearing.
In case of single Cheque Bounce, dealer needs to work only on
Electronic Mode
Cheque facility is totally at the discretion of the company and can be withdraw
at any time with prior information to channel partner.
Date of cheque return as accounted in Company books will be considered for
all the above clauses.
Company reserves the right to vary/amend/ add or withdraw any of the above
policies (Segment) at any point of time without prior intimation.
This Policy comes into effect from 30th March, 2026
THIS POLICY SUPERSEDES ALL EARLIER POLICIES/CIRCULARS/
SANCTIONS AND UNDERSTANDING.

30 March 2026
CEAT DISTRIBUTION POLICY for FY26-27
1. Appointment of 2/3 wheeler, PCR/UVR, LCV & LM distributor:
a. Distributor can be appointed only with the prior approval of ZM of respective
zone.
b. All Distributor accounts will be treated as an Individual accounts for
implementation of this Policy.
c. Distributors can be billed all categories except truck.
2. Essentials for appointment of an Ideal distributor:
a. Should deal with CEAT exclusively as far as tyre products are concerned.
b. Should operate only within demarcated area / assigned territory.
c. Should expand the retail universe and seek alternate channels including
battery, spare parts, lube, OE & Fuel stations.
d. Should have delivery van and provide exclusive salesperson for sales. To
ensure delivery of orders next day of order placement
e. Should deploy one salesperson for every 75 outlets for local and 50 outlets for
upcountry.
f. Should provide handheld/tab as per requirement of company to every
salesperson for taking order through device only.
g. Should be willing to service small outlets in the given geography and have route
/ week wise beat plan.
d. Must ensure that all the invoicing happens through CEAT Distribution
Management System (DMS) as prescribed from time to time by the company.
Ensure secondary billing is done daily.
e. Must ensure that for all the sub-dealers - complete market outstanding
collection and reconciliation is done on CEAT DMS
f. Must ensure 100% Spot Resolution of claim tyres at Distributor point (which
are not disposed by the sub dealers)
h. Should have deposit to ensure 100% adherence to daily ordered generated
through VMI system.
i. Should operate within stock norm of 15 days of monthly sale.
j. Should extend maximum of 21 days of market credit with PPD offer of 7 days
& 15 days to keep average credit of 15 days
k. Mandatory physical stock check by CEAT personnel every calendar month end.

l. Should circulate ROP and ensure strict adherence.
m. Should not have retail counter for tyres.
n. Must ensure all outlets serviced by the distributor are KYC compliant and valid
address and contact number is maintained and updated to the company.
3. Security Deposit:
All Distributors dealing with the Company will have to maintain Security Deposit with
the Company. The minimum-Security Deposit amount is Rs. 7.5 lac.
Interest on Security Deposit Scheme (SDS):
Distributors will be entitled to SDS interest of 8% (Eight percent) per annum
subject to below clauses:
a. Deduction from interest payable will be made for all Overdue Payment for the
no. of days delayed, through debit note @8% with applicable GST. The Debit
note amount will be auto adjusted against SDS interest amount.
b. If the cheque returns are more than 5% of the turnover in a quarter, no
Interest will be paid for that Quarter.
c. Interest will be paid in a particular quarter on actual SDS or average post tax
turnover value, whichever is LOWER.
The SDS Interest will be payable through Quarterly Credit Note, after deduction
of TDS.
Interest on SDS will be computed on actual deposit in company books, calculated
based on daily balance.
PAN number is mandatory for SDS interest payment to the Distributors.
Please note that Company reserves the right to revise interest rates on
SDS according to prevailing market conditions
4. Credit Limit:
The Distributor will have credit limit in the ratio of 1:1 to their security deposit. This
means that, at any point of time the total outstanding of the distributor cannot exceed
the security deposit amount.
5. Credit Period & Mode of Collection:
Type of Distributor Credit Period
Local 27 days

Upcountry 30 days
The credit period will start from the date of dispatch or 48 hours from the date of
Invoice, whichever is earlier.
All new distributors shall be on electronic mode of collection only.
All cheques will be deposited in the bank as per due date. Distributors will be given
benefit of 10 CEAT regional holidays in computation of due date. If due date falls
on any of these 10 holidays, the due date will be extended by 1 day.
6. Prompt Payment Discount (PPD):
Distributors will be eligible for PPD on all payments made as per below table. The
period mentioned below will start from the date of dispatch or 48 hours from the
date of invoice, whichever is earlier.
PPD (%) Local(period) Upcountry(period)
1.75% 7 days 10 days
1.25% 15 days 18 days
PPD will be computed as % of Pre Tax value on payment received / credit note /
SDS adjusted against the invoice. It will be paid even on partial adjustment of
invoices if adjusted within the credit period.
Mode of Disbursement – Monthly Credit Note
All the Distributors making payment through channel finance will get 1.75 % PPD
on BOI (body of invoice).
Sludge, NOE, warranty, claim & Test Tyre invoices are excluded from PPD entitlement.
7. Adjustment of Overdue / Debit note:
Any invoice or part thereof, outstanding beyond 30 days from the due date will be
adjusted against SDS interest followed by security deposit.
Similarly, any debit notes generated by the company on the dealer shall be liable to
be paid and any such outstanding beyond 30 days from the date of debit note will be
adjusted against SDS interest followed by security deposit.
8. Distributor Discount:

Distributor Discount of 3.75% will be paid to distributors on the body of the invoice
on the total product value before tax.
9. Subsidy for DSE:
All new distributors based at hilly terrain of North East, Uttarakhand, Himachal
Pradesh, Konkan, J&K and far-flung areas of Rajasthan will be paid a subsidy of
Rs.7,000 towards appointment of exclusive salesperson till the business turnover
reaches to Rs.24 lacs per month or 6 months whichever is earlier.
In the event a distributor is unable to reach desired value turnover in 6 months,
company will have the right to terminate the distributor.
10. Cheque Return:
If there is cheque return by a Distributor, 0.5% of the cheque value or Rs. 5,000,
whichever is higher, will be debited to the Distributors through debit note.
The payment of the returned cheque must be done through RTGS/Online Mode only
& no bounced cheque will be represented in bank for clearing.
All the customers appointed in FY26 and beyond will be on electronic mode
of payment only.
In case of single Cheque Bounce (from FY26), customer needs to work only
on Electronic Mode
Cheque facility is totally on the discretion of the company and can be withdraw at any
time with prior information to customer.
Date of cheque return as accounted in Company books will be considered for all the
above clauses.
Company reserves the right to vary/amend/ add or withdraw any of the above policies
(Segment) at any point of time without prior intimation.
This Policy comes into effect from 30th March 2026 and supersedes all earlier
policies/circulars/sanctions and understandings.
